title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You recently had a very bad experience travelling on a long-distance train. The journey was delayed, the facilities were poor, and the staff were unhelpful. Write a letter of complaint to the train company, describing the problems you experienced and requesting compensation.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Customer Relations Team,

I am writing to make a formal complaint about a journey I undertook on your service on the 14th of March, travelling from Bristol to Edinburgh (Booking Reference: TRN-77452). I am afraid the experience fell far below any acceptable standard.

The train departed over two hours late with no explanation or apology offered at any point. During the delay, passengers received no information from staff whatsoever, despite asking repeatedly. When the train eventually arrived, I discovered that the heating in my carriage was not functioning, making the four-hour journey deeply uncomfortable. The onboard cafe was also closed for the entire journey, leaving passengers without food or drink.

When I approached a member of staff to raise these concerns, I was told simply to contact customer services online — which I found unhelpful given the circumstances.

I would like to request a full refund of my ticket price of £87, along with a formal written apology. I hope to hear from you within fourteen days.

Yours sincerely,
Fiona MacGregor

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'We are collecting reviews of travel apps or transport services. Have you used an app or service that made travelling easier? Write a review describing it and saying whether you would recommend it to travellers.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
App Review: Citymapper — The Traveller's Best Friend

Citymapper is a public transport navigation app that has genuinely transformed how I travel in cities. Available for dozens of major cities worldwide, it combines data from buses, trains, metros, cycling routes, and walking to give you the fastest and most practical route between any two points.

What distinguishes Citymapper from standard map apps is its real-time accuracy. It updates instantly when services are delayed, suggests alternative routes, and even tells you which door of the train to board for the quickest exit. For anyone unfamiliar with a city's transport system, it is an absolute lifesaver.

I used it extensively during a recent trip to London and found it flawless. It handled complex connections involving multiple modes of transport effortlessly and even factored in walking time between interchanges.

The app is free, well-designed, and works offline once you have downloaded your chosen city. The only limitation is that it covers major cities only — rural transport options are not available.

For city travel, Citymapper is without question the best app available. Rating: 5/5

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your local council has commissioned a report on transport in your area, focusing on issues faced by young people. Include information about the main transport problems young people experience, the impact these have on daily life, and recommendations for improvement.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Transport Issues Affecting Young People in Castleford

Introduction
This report presents findings from a survey of 140 young residents aged 16–25 regarding the transport challenges they face in Castleford, and makes recommendations for improvements.

Main Transport Problems
The most commonly cited issue was the high cost of bus and rail travel. Many respondents noted that a monthly transport pass consumes a significant proportion of their income or student loan. Infrequent evening and weekend services were also flagged as a major problem, particularly affecting those who work or socialise outside normal hours. Several respondents in outlying villages reported having virtually no reliable public transport at all.

Impact on Daily Life
Poor transport access was linked to difficulties accessing employment, education, and social activities. Several young people reported turning down job offers because they could not reliably get to work.

Recommendations
The council should introduce an affordable youth travel pass, increase evening and weekend service frequency on key routes, and explore subsidised taxi or ride-sharing schemes for residents in areas with limited bus coverage. These changes would meaningfully improve economic and social opportunities for young people.
